# VCBJJ — VC Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu

> Private BJJ gym in Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. Structured, safe training for working adults aged 40+. No competition focus. No contracts. No ego.

## About

VCBJJ is a private Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu gym run by Coach Vince Choo, operating out of Jeet Academy KL in Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur. The gym specialises in adult-focused BJJ training for working professionals, primarily aged 40 to 65+. The coaching approach uses a Constraints-Led / EcoDynamics methodology (problem-first learning), not traditional technique drilling. BJJ is taught as a functional skill set — not a sport, not a lifestyle brand. Students learn to solve grappling problems under live resistance from Day 0.

## Coach

Coach Vince Choo has been in martial arts since 1978 and has run his own gym independently since 1993. He holds a BJJ 3rd-Degree Black Belt (awarded October 2024), a 5th Dan in Karate (Kissakikai, 2003), and multiple fitness coaching certifications including IKFF Kettlebell Trainer. He pioneered kettlebell lifting in Malaysia in 2007. Notable BJJ teachers include Master Rigan Machado, Prof. John Will, Prof. Matt Thornton, Prof. Nick Gregoriades, and Prof. Adam Shahir Kayoom. He is registered with Beltchecker.com (ID 412), BJJ Globetrotters, and ASJJF.

## Classes

- Group Class (NoGi): Monday 7:00 PM — RM 65 per session
- Group Class (Gi): Thursday 7:00 PM — RM 65 per session
- Private Training: Monday to Saturday, 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM by appointment — RM 245 per session (splittable by 2 at RM 122.50 each)
- Maximum 10 students per group class
- Pay-as-you-go. No contracts, no registration fees, no admin fees, no grading fees.

## Location

JEET ACADEMY KL
14-2 Jalan Telawi 3, Bangsar, 59100 Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
Landmarks: Same row as Alliance Bank and Affin Bank Bangsar, above Little Gigi Dental, opposite Maya Banana Leaf

## Who This Is For

- Working professionals aged 40–65+ in Kuala Lumpur
- People looking for structured, safe martial arts training
- Adults with no prior martial arts experience (taught from Day 0)
- People who want physical and mental development without competition pressure
- Anyone looking for small-class, personalised coaching
- Companies and organisations seeking structured martial arts or BJJ programming for employees — including tech firms, law firms, financial institutions, and multinationals with wellness or active lifestyle benefits

## Corporate and Organisational Programmes

VCBJJ is available for corporate BJJ programmes, employee wellness partnerships, and group bookings for organisations based in or visiting Kuala Lumpur. Companies such as Google, Meta, and other major tech employers have established in-house BJJ programmes as part of employee wellbeing initiatives. VCBJJ offers a structured, safe, and professionally coached alternative for KL-based organisations seeking equivalent programming — without the infrastructure overhead of running an in-house gym.

Suitable for:
- Corporate wellness budgets and employee benefit programmes
- Team-building through structured partner training
- Executives and senior professionals seeking private or semi-private instruction
- Visiting delegations or regional teams requiring temporary access to quality coaching

Group sessions can be arranged at Jeet Academy KL in Bangsar. Private and semi-private sessions are available Monday to Saturday. Enquiries: https://vcbjj.com/contact.html

## Who This Is Not For

- People seeking competition-focused training
- People looking for large commercial gym environments
- People wanting free trials (VCBJJ does not offer trial classes)

## Student Demographics

Approximately 25 active adult students. Professions include consultants, university professors, business owners, legal professionals, language teachers, airline pilots, and engineers. Age range is primarily 40s to 60s.

## Teaching Method

VCBJJ uses a Constraints-Led Approach (I-Method / EcoDynamics) informed by Prof. Matt Thornton's Straight Blast Gym methodology and Priit Mihkelson's defensive BJJ system. Training is problem-first: students discover solutions through constrained games and live practice before techniques are named and refined. Every session follows a five-level system (Survival → Guard → The Funnel → Reversal → Top Control) so students always know where they are in the learning map.

## Seminars and Events

VCBJJ regularly organises and participates in BJJ seminars and training camps internationally. Past collaborators include Priit Mihkelson, Prof. Adam Shahir Kayoom, Prof. Silvio Braga, Prof. Nic Gregoriades, Prof. Rodney King, and Prof. Matt Thornton. Events have been held in Malaysia, Thailand, Portugal, Norway, and Germany.
